title: symphytum grandiflorum
intro: Symphytum grandiflorum, also known as Comfrey, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The medicinal properties of Comfrey lie in its high content of allantoin, a compound that promotes healing and tissue repair, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Comfrey include its ability to soothe and calm skin irritations, reduce swelling and pain, and aid in the healing of wounds and broken bones. Comfrey is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in ointments and creams to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
